LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. WHEN A GOVERNMENT ENTITY SEEKS TO ENJOIN VIOLATIONS OF LOCAL STATUTES THAT SPECIFICALLY PROVIDE FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AS A MEANS OF ENFORCEMENT, THE GOVERNMENT NEED ONLY ESTABLISH A PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. ITT CORP. VS. COUNTY OF IMPERIAL (1983) 35 CAL. 3D 63. A REBUTTABLE PRESUMPTION ARISES THAT THE BALANCE OF HARM FAVORS THE PUBLIC INTEREST OVER THE PRIVATE INTEREST, AND THE BURDEN SHIFTS TO CROSS-DEFENDANT TO PROVE IRREPARABLE HARM. THE CITY SUES PURSUANT TO S.F. PLANNING CODE SECTIONS 176 AND 790.36, AND IS AUTHORIZED TO SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FOR ANY VIOLATION OF THE PLANNING CODE. THE CITY ESTABLISHED THAT IT IS REASONABLY LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS. THE CEASE AND DESIST ORDERS AND NOTICE OF VIOLATION ESTABLISH THAT THE CLUB WAS OPERATING WITHOUT A PERMIT. THE DELANEY DECLARATION SUPPORTS A CITATION FOR PROSTITUTION AT THE CLUB, GAINES SAW FEMALES DANCING ON AN ELEVATED POLE, AND A FLYER ADVERTIZED "TOTALLY NUDE DANCES." CROSS-DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW IRREPARABLE HARM IF AN INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S CLAIM OF ECONOMIC HARM IS COMPENSABLE IF THEY PREVAIL AT TRIAL AND THE PROPERTY MAY BE USED IN A MANNER THAT DOES NOT VIOLATE THE INJUNCTION. ORDER RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (COUNSEL STIPULATE ON THE RECORD THAT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL ACCEPTS SERVICE FOR ALL CROSS-DEFENDANTS.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Jeffrey A. Chen, Esq., for Cross-Defendant and opposing party: Alyce Craft

Hearing held on DEFENDANT THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Cross Defendants 1054 Kearny, LLC; Alyce D.K. Craft, As Trustee; And Peter Lambertson; And Request For Monetary Sanctions.

Ntc Of Mtn For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-19-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS COMPLAINANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Frank M. Brass, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF, (415) 554-3880, appearing for Cross-Complainant and moving party; Gregory S. Walston, of Walston Cross (415) 956-9200 appearing for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendants and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Preliminary Injunction is granted. When a government entity seeks to enjoin violations of local statutes that specifically provide for injunctive relief as a means of enforcement, the government need only establish a probability of prevailing on the merits. ITT Corp. vs. County of Imperial (1983) 35 Cal. 3d 63. A rebuttable presumption arises that the balance of harm favors the public interest over the private interest, and the burden shifts to cross-defendant to prove irreparable harm. The City sues pursuant to S.F. Planning Code sections 176 and 790.36, and is authorized to seek injunctive relief for any violation of the Planning Code. The City established that it is reasonably likely to prevail on the merits. The cease and desist orders and notice of violation establish that the club was operating without a permit. The Delaney declaration supports a citation for prostitution at the club, Gaines saw females dancing on an elevated pole, and a flyer advertized "Totally Nude Dances." Cross-defendant fails to show irreparable harm if an injunction is granted. Cross-defendant's claim of economic harm is compensable if they prevail at trial and the property may be used in a manner that does not violate the injunction. Order re: Preliminary Injunction signed in Open court. (Counsel stipulate on the record that Plaintiff's counsel accepts service for all Cross-Defendants.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
